name: LinkedIn MCP Server
what_it_does: LinkedIn MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with LinkedIn — posting content and articles, searching for jobs and professionals, managing connections, and integrating LinkedIn's professional network into agent-driven personal branding, recruiting, and professional networking workflows.
use_cases: Posting LinkedIn content and articles from personal branding agents
Searching for job listings from career management agents
Finding and researching professionals from recruiting agents
Analyzing company pages and news from market research agents
Managing LinkedIn outreach from networking automation agents
Generating LinkedIn post drafts from content creation agents
not_for: Mass automated outreach (violates LinkedIn ToS; can result in account ban)
Teams needing Twitter/X or Instagram (use those specific MCPs)
Data scraping at scale (LinkedIn actively blocks automated scraping)
best_when: An agent needs to post content or perform limited professional networking tasks on LinkedIn — content publishing, job searching, or light research within ToS limits.
avoid_when: You need mass outreach or scraping — LinkedIn's ToS strictly prohibits automation at scale and enforces with account bans.

auth.notes: LinkedIn OAuth 2.0 required. LinkedIn developer app registration needed. Access token via OAuth flow. LinkedIn app must be approved for required permissions (marketing, shares, etc.). Setup is complex.

pricing.free_tier_limits: LinkedIn API: basic profile and post API free for approved apps
pricing.paid_tiers: LinkedIn Marketing API requires partner agreement for advanced features
pricing.notes: LinkedIn API access requires developer app approval. Basic sharing and profile read is free. Advanced marketing APIs require LinkedIn partnership agreement. Community MCP is free.

performance.rate_limits: LinkedIn API: 100 calls/day (basic); higher on approved partner programs

known_agent_gotchas: LinkedIn OAuth setup requires developer app registration and approval — complex process
LinkedIn API rate limits are very restrictive (100 calls/day basic) — easy to exhaust
LinkedIn actively monitors and restricts automated behavior — ToS violations risk account ban
API permissions require LinkedIn to manually approve your app for specific scopes
Community MCP — LinkedIn API changes frequently; server may lag official API changes
Never automate mass outreach or connection requests — this will result in LinkedIn account suspension
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. OAuth 2.0. GDPR. Community MCP. Store OAuth tokens securely. Respect LinkedIn ToS strictly — violations result in permanent account bans. Do not automate mass messaging.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS. OAuth 2.0. GDPR. Store OAuth tokens securely. Respect ToS — violations = account ban.
